Items.Title: 作为开发人员，您在 2022 年的首要任务是什么？ | What is your top priority as a developer in 2022?
Items.Description:
开发团队的Ben Halpern

发表于这是 DEV 和Mayfield之间设计的一系列大问题中的第一篇。

从这个开始，请每天参与一个新的线程。 经过 10 次讨论后，我们将总结一些讨论并发布关键要点。

快乐编码！



原文:

Ben Halpern for The DEV Team

Posted on May 9

This is the first post in a series of big questions devised between DEV and Mayfield.

Please feel welcome to take part in a new thread every day starting with this one. After a 10 discussions we will be rounding up some of the discussions and publishing key takeaways.

Happy coding!

Items.Title: 音乐星期一——你在听什么？ (May 9) （5月9日） | Music Monday — What are you listening to? (May 9)
Items.Description:
封面图片来源： DLGNCE

在这个每周系列中，人们可以加入并删除他们正在收听的任何内容的链接 + 浏览其他建议。

如果您喜欢谈论音乐，请考虑关注 org #music 讨论以获取更多关于音乐的对话！

同样值得注意的是，我的朋友@duxtech在这里有这个系列的西班牙语版本，以防你也想插话。 那么，你们今天都在听什么？ 注意：您可以使用以下语法在大多数平台上嵌入指向您的歌曲的链接{% embed https://... %} 。

期待听听大家的建议！ 技术世界与娜娜 - Apr 6 4月6日



原文:

cover image credits: DLGNCE

In this weekly series, folks can chime in and drop links to whatever it is they're listening to + browse others suggestions.

If you like talking about music, consider following the org #music discussions for more conversations about music!

Also noteworthy, my friend @duxtech has a Spanish speaking version of this series here in case you'd like to chime in there as well. 🙌

So, what have y'all been listening to today? Note: you can embed a link to your song on most platforms using the following syntax {% embed https://... %}.

I look forward to listening to y'all's suggestions! 👂 🎶

TechWorld with Nana - Apr 6

Items.Title: 在几分钟内为您的网站创建最快的搜索，没有任何依赖 ⚡🔎 | Create the fastest search for your website in minutes, without any dependencies ⚡🔎
Items.Description:
我正在处理的项目是用 Gatsby JS 编写的，但解决方案本身是 vanilla react 并且可以在任何地方工作。

我正在处理的项目是用 Gatsby JS 编写的，但解决方案本身是 vanilla react 并且可以在任何地方工作。

今天，我大部分时间都在更新我的博客，并考虑添加更多功能，如搜索、标签、MDX 支持，以及一些设计更改，包括侧边栏。

我正在决定如何实现搜索功能，因为我唯一一次这样做是使用
但是，托管服务器端的成本很高，尤其是对于
像博客这样简单的东西。 他们的托管解决方案在价格上也不是那么好。

所以有一件事是肯定的，没有必要为此使用任何 API。 在谷歌快速搜索之后，我在 Gatsby 的网站上看到了这个文档，它是关于向 Gatsby 添加搜索的

在该指南的Client Side部分下，这是他们的建议：

无需第三方解决方案即可在您的 Gatsby 站点中完成所有工作。 这涉及编写一些代码，但使用较少的服务。 如果要索引大量内容，它还可以显着增加捆绑包的大小。

一种方法是使用 js-search 库：

使用 JS 搜索添加搜索

有两个 Gatsby 插件也支持这一点：

gatsby-plugin-elasticlunr-search
gatsby-plugin-local-search

现在，这些搜索方法索引所有内容，这意味着更大的捆绑包大小。 而且它们的设置也很麻烦。

我采用的解决方案

现在对于我的用例，我自己做一些简单的事情可能是个好主意，我可以在我不断更新这个博客的同时继续构建它。

这个想法很简单，我只需要制作一个搜索框，在每次击键时，循环浏览内容并像这样过滤它们。

 const BlogIndex = ({ data , location }) => { // These posts can be anything, // I've just used the posts from a gatsby query const posts = data . allMdx . edges ; // We need to filter the posts by the search query. // by default, we have all posts const [ filteredPosts , setFilteredPosts ] = useState ( posts ); // This will be the search query const [ search , setSearch ] = useState ( '' ); return ( < div > { /* Our search bar */ } < input type = "text" placeholder = "Search" onChange = { ( e ) => { e . preventDefault (); setSearch ( e . target . value )} } /> { /* Simply mapping through everything and rendering blogs */ } { filteredPosts . map (({ node }) => { < BlogPost post = { node } key = { node . id } / > } } </ div > ) }

现在，只要在框中输入内容， search状态就会更新。 现在，让我们编写一个useEffect钩子来在search状态发生变化时更新filteredPosts后的状态。

 const BlogIndex = ({ data , location }) => { const posts = data . allMdx . edges ; const [ filteredPosts , setFilteredPosts ] = useState ( posts ); const [ search , setSearch ] = useState ( '' ); //highlight-start useEffect (() => { if ( search ) { // post filtering here } } // only update the filteredPosts state when the search state changes or the posts state changes , [ search , posts ]); ///highlight-end return ( ... // rest of the code )

现在让我们编写一些非常简单的代码来过滤帖子。

 ... if ( search ) { const filteredPosts = posts . filter ( post => { //highlight-start const title = post . title . toLowerCase (); const description = post . description . toLowerCase (); return title . match ( search . toLowerCase ()) || description . match ( search . toLowerCase ()); //highlight-end } } setFilteredPosts ( filteredPosts ); ...

由于我的博客有标签和类似的东西，我也添加了按标签搜索和过滤的功能

if ( search . startsWith ( " # " )) { return tags . includes ( search . replace ( " # " , "" )); } ...

就是这样！ 但是等等，还有更多。 这可行，但您不能真正将搜索查询分享给其他人，我可以分享谷歌链接 - google.com/search?q=github
我认为这很重要，例如，当我必须分享我所有的 Rust 博客时，它更容易和方便。

好吧，让我们实时更新 URL 以包含搜索查询！ 我以前从来没有这样做过，所以学习它很棒。 我从IFTTT 搜索引擎获得灵感

我发现了window.history.pushState()方法，它基本上允许您推送一个新 URL，而无需将其添加到浏览器历史记录或重新加载页面。 在此处阅读相同的文档-
历史 API | 现在，我们还需要解析原始请求，使用window location对象，并将其设置为我们为search创建的useState钩子的默认值

// 👇🏻 converts the URL from HTML encoded to a string (%20 to space) const initialState = decodeURI ( location . href ? // Use window location . split ( ' / ' ) // Split the URL into an array . pop () // Get the last element only . split ( " = " ) // at this point, it's q=search, so we only need the "Search" parth . pop () ); // 👇🏻 We're using the initialState to set the search query. const [ search , setSearch ] = useState ( initialState ); // Now, only the blogs that match the query will be displayed on first load

就是这样！

完整的实现可以在 Github 上这篇博客的源代码中找到

您可以自己尝试搜索

随意访问此博客的存储库这里



原文:

The project I'm working on is written in Gatsby JS, but the solution itself is vanilla react and will work everywhere.

Jump to main content

The project I'm working on is written in Gatsby JS, but the solution itself is vanilla react and will work everywhere.

Today, I spent most of my time updating my blog, and thought to add more features like search, tags, MDX support, and a few design changes, including the sidebar.

I was deciding how I would implement the search function, because the only time I have done it, was using a
Self hosted version of Typesense

But well, that was costly to host server-side, especially for
something as simple as a blog. and their hosted solutions aren't that great price-wise either.

So one thing was sure, there is no need to use any API for this. After a quick google search, I came across this documentation on Gatsby's website which is about adding search to Gatsby

From that guide, under the Client Side section, here's what they recommend:

It is possible to do all the work in your Gatsby site without needing a third-party solution. This involves writing a bit of code, but using less services. With large amounts of content to index, it can also increase the bundle size significantly.

One way of doing this is to use the js-search library:

Adding Search with JS Search

There are two Gatsby plugins that support this as well:

gatsby-plugin-elasticlunr-search
gatsby-plugin-local-search

Now these search methods index everything which means higher bundle size. And they are also a hassle to set up.

The solution I went with

Now for my use case, it was probably a good idea to just make something simple by myself, and I can build on it as I keep updating this blog.

The idea is really simple, I just need to make a search box, and on every keystroke, loop through the contents and filter them like that.

const BlogIndex = ({ data, location }) => { // These posts can be anything, // I've just used the posts from a gatsby query const posts = data.allMdx.edges; // We need to filter the posts by the search query. // by default, we have all posts const [filteredPosts, setFilteredPosts] = useState(posts); // This will be the search query const [search, setSearch] = useState(''); return ( <div> {/* Our search bar */} <input type="text" placeholder="Search" onChange={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); setSearch(e.target.value)} }/> {/* Simply mapping through everything and rendering blogs */} {filteredPosts.map(({ node }) => { <BlogPost post={node} key={node.id} />
 }} </div> )
}

Now, whenever something is typed in the box, the search state will be updated. Now, let's write a useEffect hook to update the filteredPosts state whenever the search state changes.

const BlogIndex = ({ data, location }) => { const posts = data.allMdx.edges; const [filteredPosts, setFilteredPosts] = useState(posts); const [search, setSearch] = useState(''); //highlight-start useEffect(() => { if (search) { // post filtering here } } // only update the filteredPosts state when the search state changes or the posts state changes , [search, posts]); ///highlight-end return ( ... // rest of the code )

And now let's write some very simple code to filter the posts.

... if (search) { const filteredPosts = posts.filter(post => { //highlight-start const title = post.title.toLowerCase(); const description = post.description.toLowerCase(); return title.match(search.toLowerCase()) || description.match(search.toLowerCase()); //highlight-end } } setFilteredPosts(filteredPosts);
...

Since my blog has tags and stuff like that, I added functionality to search and filter by tags, too

 if (search.startsWith("#")) { return tags.includes(search.replace("#", "")); }
...

And that's it! But wait, there's more. This works, but you can't really share a search query to someone else, I can share google links - google.com/search?q=github
I think that's kinda important, like, for times when I have to share all my Rust blogs, it's just easier and convenient.

so well, let's update the URL to include the search query, in real time! I hadn't ever done this before, so it was great learning it. I got the inspiration from the IFTTT search engine

I found out about the window.history.pushState() method, which basically allows you to push a new URL without adding it to the browser history, or reloading the page. Read the documentation for the same over here -
History API | MDN

useEffect(() => { if (search) { //highlight-start if (window.history.pushState) { window.history.pushState(null, null, `/?q=${search}`); } //highlight-end const filteredPosts = posts.filter(post => { const title = post.title.toLowerCase(); const description = post.description.toLowerCase(); return title.match(search.toLowerCase()) || description.match(search.toLowerCase()); } } setFilteredPosts(filteredPosts); }, [search]);

And now, we also need to parse the original request, using the window location object, and make it default for the useState hook we made for search

 // 👇🏻 converts the URL from HTML encoded to a string (%20 to space) const initialState = decodeURI(location.href? // Use window location .split('/') // Split the URL into an array .pop() // Get the last element only .split("=") // at this point, it's q=search, so we only need the "Search" parth .pop() ); // 👇🏻 We're using the initialState to set the search query. const [search, setSearch] = useState(initialState); // Now, only the blogs that match the query will be displayed on first load

That's it!

The full implementation can be found in the source code of this blog on Github

You can try out the search yourself

Feel free to visit the repository for this blog here
Items.Author: dhravya

Items.Title: 构建和 ​​Docker 化 Go 应用程序的指南 | The Guide to Build and Dockerize a Go App
Items.Description:
优胜者 Musole Masu

发表于大家好，欢迎来到我们的教程。 动词 Dockerize 对某些人来说可能听起来很陌生，只是为了和谐，动词只是意味着我们将使用 Docker 容器打包、部署和运行我们的 Go 应用程序。

容器是打包代码及其所有依赖项的软件单元，因此应用程序可以从一个计算环境快速可靠地运行到另一个计算环境。 换句话说，构建我们的 Go 应用程序所需的所有工具将与应用程序本身堆叠在一起，形成一个可移植的标准模块； 它可以在任何地方访问并在各种操作系统上运行。

先决条件

本教程的第一个先决条件是具备 Go 编程的基本知识，其次，具备以下条件：

  • 安装运行时，
  • 这个应用程序将成为一个获取多家上市公司股票市场信息的 API。 它将向 Alpha Vantage API 发送 GET 请求，这是接收股票数据的最有效方式。

    如上述场景所设计，客户端将向本地机器中运行的服务器发出带有查询参数的 GET 请求。 然后服务器将使用该查询参数向 Alpha Vantage API 发出另一个 GET 请求，该 API 将返回与查询相关的股票数据。

    注意：打开此链接以注册 Rapid API 并订阅 Alpha Vantage API。

    现在，让我们深入研究编码。 首先，创建一个名为stock-list的目录并移入：

     $ mkdir stock-list $ cd stock-list/

    运行此命令来管理内部包或依赖项：

    并运行这些命令来安装本教程中使用的依赖项：

     $ go get github.com/gin-gonic/gin $ go get github.com/rodaine/table

    创建一个名为stock.go的文件，打开它并将代码片段放在下面：

     package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "io/ioutil" "net/http" "github.com/rodaine/table" ) type Quote struct { SYMBOL string `json:"01. symbol"` OPEN string `json:"02. open"` HIGH string `json:"03. high"` LOW string `json:"04. low"` PRICE string `json:"05. price"` VOLUME string `json:"06. volume"` LATEST_TRADING_DAY string `json:"07. latest trading day"` PREVIOUS_CLOSE string `json:"08. previous close"` CHANGE string `json:"09. change"` CHANGE_PERCENT string `json:"10. change percent"` } type Stock struct { DETAIL Quote `json:"Global Quote"` } func getStock ( s string , ch chan Stock ) Stock { var stock Stock api := "https://alpha-vantage.p.rapidapi.com/query?function=GLOBAL_QUOTE&symbol=" + s + "&datatype=json" req , _ := http . NewRequest ( "GET" , api , nil ); req . Header . Add ( "X-RapidAPI-Host" , "alpha-vantage.p.rapidapi.com" ) req . Header . Add ( "X-RapidAPI-Key" , "Here put your RapidAPI Key" ) res , err := http . DefaultClient . Do ( req ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( "No response from request" ) } defer res . Body . Close () body , _ := ioutil . ReadAll ( res . Body ) if err := json . Unmarshal ( body , & stock ); err != nil { fmt . Println ( "Can not unmarshal JSON" ) } ch <- stock return stock } func genTable ( watchList [] Quote ){ tbl := table . New ( "SYMBOL" , "OPEN" , "HIGH" , "LOW" , "PRICE" , "VOLUME" , "PREVIOUS-CLOSE" , "CHANGE" ) for _ , stock := range watchList { tbl . AddRow ( stock . SYMBOL , stock . OPEN , stock . HIGH , stock . LOW , stock . PRICE , stock . VOLUME , stock . PREVIOUS_CLOSE , stock . CHANGE ) } tbl . Print () }

    在上面的代码中，我们做了以下事情：
    这部分文件导入fmt将字符串打印到控制台， github.com/rodaine/table /rodaine/table 从股票数据生成表， net/http用于向 Alpha Vantage API 发出 HTTP 请求， io/ioutil用于读取来自 Alpha Vantage 的响应正文的库存数据和encoding/json以将 json 库存数据（在 []byte 中）转换为我们的对象结构。

    这个函数有两个参数，一个只是股票代码的字符串和一个 Stock 结构类型的通道。 然后它从 Alpha Vantage API 检索该股票代码的股票数据。

    注意：确保在指示处添加您的 RapidAPI 密钥。

    复制下面屏幕一侧指示的密钥：


    并将其粘贴在这里：


    现在您已经将您的 Rapid API 密钥粘贴到适当的位置，让我们继续此功能说明。 在两个序列中，我们执行了以下操作：

    序列 1 ：
    该函数使用 http 包向 Alpha Vantage API 发出 GET 请求，它在请求中添加了两个标头参数，其中携带包含我们客户端对所请求股票数据的身份验证信息的凭据。 序列 2 ：
    该函数使用io/ioutil包从 Alpha Vantage API 读取响应正文，将正文从 []byte 的 json 转换为具有json.unmarshal的股票结构类型，并通过通道将转换后的股票数据返回到主线程ch<- stock 。

    它将为数组中的每个项目生成一行。

    在同一文件夹/stock-list ，添加一个名为main.go的 go 文件并放入以下代码：

     package main import ( "strings" "github.com/gin-gonic/gin" ) func main () { var watchList [] Quote ch := make ( chan Stock ) router := gin . Default () router . GET ( "/stocks/" , func ( c * gin . Context ){ stockSymbol := c . Query ( "symbol" ) listOfSymbol := strings . Split ( stockSymbol , "," ) for _ , symbol := range listOfSymbol { go getStock ( symbol , ch ) } for i := 0 ; i < len ( listOfSymbol ); i ++ { result := <- ch watchList = append ( watchList , Quote { SYMBOL : result . DETAIL . SYMBOL , OPEN : result . DETAIL . OPEN , HIGH : result . DETAIL . HIGH , LOW : result . DETAIL . LOW , PRICE : result . DETAIL . PRICE , VOLUME : result . DETAIL . VOLUME , LATEST_TRADING_DAY : result . DETAIL . LATEST_TRADING_DAY , PREVIOUS_CLOSE : result . DETAIL . PREVIOUS_CLOSE , CHANGE : result . DETAIL . CHANGE , CHANGE_PERCENT : result . DETAIL . CHANGE_PERCENT , }) } genTable ( watchList ) }) router . Run ( "localhost:8000" ) }

    让我们分解上面的代码；
    在这部分代码中，导入了strings包以覆盖字符串方法和github.com/gin-gonic/gin或 Gin，这是一个简单地处理路由的 HTTP Web 框架。

    这是可执行程序的入口点。 在那里，我们声明了一个 struct Quote 类型的数组watchList ，股票数据将附加到该数组中。 接下来，我们使用ch := make(chan Stock)创建了一个通道来进行主线程和子线程之间的通信。

    此处理程序使用 Gin 包获取带有stockSymbol := c.Query("symbol")的查询参数，并且由于查询可能包含多个用逗号分隔的字符串参数，因此我们使用listOfSymbol := strings.Split(stockSymbol, ",") 。

    然后对于每个参数（股票代码），我们同时向 Alpha Vantage API 发出 GET 请求：

     for _ , symbol := range listOfSymbol { go getStock ( symbol , ch ) }

    接下来，对于每个 GET 请求，Alpha Vantage 将股票数据的响应发送回go getStock(symbol, ch)作为子线程，通过通道传递到主线程。 在主线程中，我们将股票数据存储在watchList数组中，并使用genTable(watchList)生成一个表。

    我们的项目目录应该类似于下面的项目树：

     . ├── go.mod ├── go.sum ├── main.go └── stock.go 0 directories, 4 files

    在本教程的这一部分，我们将对新建的 Go 应用程序进行 docker 化。 为此，请留在工作目录中，创建一个名为Dockerfile的文件，将以下代码放入其中：

     FROM golang:1.18-alpine WORKDIR /app COPY go.mod ./ COPY go.sum ./ RUN go mod download COPY *.go ./ RUN go build -o /stock-list CMD [ "/stock-list" ]

    它带有所有工具和包来编译和运行我们的应用程序。

    成功创建 Dockerfile 后，让我们从中构建一个 docker 镜像。

    注意：在此之前，请确保拥有Docker Hub 帐户并使用其凭据，在本地计算机上使用docker login命令登录。

    在项目目录/stock-list ，在以下命令中使用您的 Docker Hub ID 来构建映像：

     $ docker build --tag <YOUR DOCKER HUB ID>/stock-list .

    通过运行以下命令可视化 stock-list docker 图像：

    运行股票列表图像：

     $ docker run -p 8080:8080 <YOUR DOCKER HUB ID>/stock-list

    为了查看我们正在运行的镜像或容器，让我们运行以下命令：


运行该命令后，我们可以看到 stock-list 镜像的 docker 容器暴露给了你本地机器上的 8080 0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp 。

要查看 Go 应用程序是否正常工作，我们将使用 curl 向服务器发出 GET 请求：

 $ curl http://localhost:8080/stocks/?symbol = TSLA,TWTR,AAPL

在此处查找更多股票代码，以进行更多测试。

此外，在我们的例子中，我们可以使用不同的其他命令来停止容器（运行 stock-list 映像的容器）、重新启动或删除它。

要停止容器，让我们在这里取容器 ID 或容器名称；
将其粘贴到以下命令中：

 $ docker stop <YOUR CONTAINER ID>

要重新启动，我们可以通过以下命令使用相同的容器 ID 或容器名称：

 $ docker restart <YOUR CONTAINER ID>

并且要完全删除 stock-list 图像的容器，仍然具有相同的容器 ID，让我们使用以下命令：

 $ docker rm <YOUR CONTAINER ID>

更详细的 docker 命令请查看官方文档。

这是本教程的最后一部分，我们将在其中将本地股票列表图像推送到 Docker Hub。 我们成功地构建了我们的 Go 应用程序，并将其与所有必要的依赖项打包在一个 docker 镜像中； 是时候将它上传到 Docker Hub 了。

让我们使用以下命令推送我们的图像：

 $ docker push <YOUR DOCKER HUB ID>/stock-list


这样做将使我们的股票列表图像可移植并可供其他可以在其操作系统上运行它的开发人员访问。

我们的教程到此结束，我们从头开始构建了一个 Go 应用程序，我们使用了一些外部 Go 包并对一个很棒的 AP​​I 进行了 HTTP 调用。 为了对应用程序进行 docker 化，我们从中构建了一个 docker 镜像，我们运行它并将其推送到 Docker Hub。 谢谢你们一起做这件事。

在这里找到项目代码。



原文:

Winner Musole Masu

Posted on May 10

Hey guys, welcome to our tutorial. The verb Dockerize might sound unfamiliar for some, just to be in harmony, the verb simply means that we will pack, deploy and run our Go application using Docker containers.

Containers are units of software that package up code and all its dependencies so the application runs quickly and reliably from one computing environment to another. In other words, all the necessary tools to build our Go Application will be piled together with the application itself to make one single portable and standard module; which could be accessible anywhere and run on various operating systems.

Prerequisites

The first prerequisite for this tutorial is to have a basic knowledge of Go programming and second, to have the following:

  • Docker installed on our computers,
  • Docker Hub account,
  • Go Runtime installed,
  • RapidAPI account and subscription to Alpha Vantage API

1. Go Application

This app is going to be an API that fetches stock market information of a number of listed companies. It will send a GET request to Alpha Vantage API which is the most effective way to receive stock data.

As designed in the scenario above, a client will make a GET request with a query parameter to the server running in the local machine. Then The server will take that query parameter to make another GET request now to the Alpha Vantage API, which will return stock data related to the query.

Note: Open this link to sign up with Rapid API and subscribe to Alpha Vantage API.

Now, let's dive into coding. First, create a directory named stock-list and move inside:

$ mkdir stock-list
$ cd stock-list/

Run this command to manage internal packages or dependencies:

And run these commands to install the dependencies to be used during this tutorial:

$ go get github.com/gin-gonic/gin
$ go get github.com/rodaine/table

Create a file named stock.go, open it and put the code snippet below:

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "io/ioutil" "net/http" "github.com/rodaine/table"
) type Quote struct { SYMBOL string `json:"01. symbol"` OPEN string `json:"02. open"` HIGH string `json:"03. high"` LOW string `json:"04. low"` PRICE string `json:"05. price"` VOLUME string `json:"06. volume"` LATEST_TRADING_DAY string `json:"07. latest trading day"` PREVIOUS_CLOSE string `json:"08. previous close"` CHANGE string `json:"09. change"` CHANGE_PERCENT string `json:"10. change percent"`
} type Stock struct { DETAIL Quote `json:"Global Quote"`
} func getStock(s string, ch chan Stock) Stock{ var stock Stock api := "https://alpha-vantage.p.rapidapi.com/query?function=GLOBAL_QUOTE&symbol="+s+"&datatype=json" req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", api, nil); req.Header.Add("X-RapidAPI-Host", "alpha-vantage.p.rapidapi.com") req.Header.Add("X-RapidAPI-Key", "Here put your RapidAPI Key") res, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req) if err != nil { fmt.Println("No response from request") } defer res.Body.Close() body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body) if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &stock); err != nil { fmt.Println("Can not unmarshal JSON") } ch<- stock return stock
} func genTable(watchList []Quote){ tbl := table.New("SYMBOL", "OPEN", "HIGH", "LOW", "PRICE", "VOLUME", "PREVIOUS-CLOSE", "CHANGE") for _, stock := range watchList { tbl.AddRow(stock.SYMBOL, stock.OPEN, stock.HIGH, stock.LOW, stock.PRICE, stock.VOLUME, stock.PREVIOUS_CLOSE, stock.CHANGE) } tbl.Print() }

1.1 Code overview 1

In the code above, we did the following:
1. Imported a couple of packages with

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "io/ioutil" "net/http" "github.com/rodaine/table"
)

This section of the file imports fmt that prints string to the console, github.com/rodaine/table which generates table from the stock data, net/http is used to make HTTP requests to Alpha Vantage API, io/ioutil is used to read stock data of the response body from the Alpha Vantage and encoding/json to convert the json stock data (which is in []byte) into our object struct.

2. Created structs for stock data

type Quote struct { SYMBOL string `json:"01. symbol"` OPEN string `json:"02. open"` HIGH string `json:"03. high"` LOW string `json:"04. low"` PRICE string `json:"05. price"` VOLUME string `json:"06. volume"` LATEST_TRADING_DAY string `json:"07. latest trading day"` PREVIOUS_CLOSE string `json:"08. previous close"` CHANGE string `json:"09. change"` CHANGE_PERCENT string `json:"10. change percent"`
} type Stock struct { DETAIL Quote `json:"Global Quote"`
}

The struct Quote basically represents the property value of the stock data contained in the response body returned by the Alpha Vantage. Stock struct is a nested struct that embodies the key and the value of the response.

3. Created the getStock() function

func getStock(s string, ch chan Stock) Stock{ var stock Stock api := "https://alpha-vantage.p.rapidapi.com/query?function=GLOBAL_QUOTE&symbol="+s+"&datatype=json" req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", api, nil); req.Header.Add("X-RapidAPI-Host", "alpha-vantage.p.rapidapi.com") req.Header.Add("X-RapidAPI-Key", "Here put your RapidAPI Key") res, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req) if err != nil { fmt.Println("No response from request") } defer res.Body.Close() body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body) if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &stock); err != nil { fmt.Println("Can not unmarshal JSON") } ch<- stock return stock
}

This function takes two parameters, a string which is just a stock symbol and a channel of Stock struct type. It then retrieves stock data of that stock symbol from the Alpha Vantage API.

Note: Make sure to add your RapidAPI Key where indicated.

Copy the key indicated in your side of the screen below:


And paste it here:


Now that you have pasted your Rapid API key to the appropriate place, let's carry on with this function explanation. In two sequences we did the following:

Sequence 1:
The function makes a GET request to the Alpha Vantage API using http package, it adds two header parameters to the request, which carries credentials containing the authentication information of our client for the stock data being requested. X-RapidAPI-Host and X-RapidAPI-Key.

Sequence 2:
The function reads the response body from the Alpha Vantage API with the io/ioutil package, converts the body from json of []byte to stock struct type with json.unmarshal and return the converted stock data via the channel to main thread ch<- stock.

4. Created the genTable() function:

func genTable(watchList []Quote){ tbl := table.New("SYMBOL", "OPEN", "HIGH", "LOW", "PRICE", "VOLUME", "PREVIOUS-CLOSE", "CHANGE") for _, stock := range watchList { tbl.AddRow(stock.SYMBOL, stock.OPEN, stock.HIGH, stock.LOW, stock.PRICE, stock.VOLUME, stock.PREVIOUS_CLOSE, stock.CHANGE) } tbl.Print() }

genTable() takes an array of Quote struct type as parameter and generates a table from it. It will generate a row for every item in the array.

In the same folder /stock-list, add a go file named main.go and put the following code:

package main import ( "strings" "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
) func main() { var watchList []Quote ch := make(chan Stock) router := gin.Default() router.GET("/stocks/", func (c *gin.Context){ stockSymbol := c.Query("symbol") listOfSymbol := strings.Split(stockSymbol, ",") for _, symbol := range listOfSymbol{ go getStock(symbol, ch) } for i := 0; i < len(listOfSymbol); i++ { result := <-ch watchList = append(watchList, Quote{ SYMBOL: result.DETAIL.SYMBOL, OPEN: result.DETAIL.OPEN, HIGH: result.DETAIL.HIGH, LOW: result.DETAIL.LOW, PRICE: result.DETAIL.PRICE, VOLUME: result.DETAIL.VOLUME, LATEST_TRADING_DAY: result.DETAIL.LATEST_TRADING_DAY, PREVIOUS_CLOSE: result.DETAIL.PREVIOUS_CLOSE, CHANGE: result.DETAIL.CHANGE, CHANGE_PERCENT: result.DETAIL.CHANGE_PERCENT, }) } genTable(watchList) }) router.Run("localhost:8000") }

1.2 Code overview 2

Let's break down the code above;
1. Imported packages:

package main import ( "strings" "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

In this part of the code, strings package is imported to override string methods and github.com/gin-gonic/gin or Gin, a HTTP web framework to handle routing with simplicity.

2. main() function:

func main() { var watchList []Quote ch := make(chan Stock) router := gin.Default() router.GET("/stocks/", func (c *gin.Context){ stockSymbol := c.Query("symbol") listOfSymbol := strings.Split(stockSymbol, ",") for _, symbol := range listOfSymbol{ go getStock(symbol, ch) } for i := 0; i < len(listOfSymbol); i++ { result := <-ch watchList = append(watchList, Quote{ SYMBOL: result.DETAIL.SYMBOL, OPEN: result.DETAIL.OPEN, HIGH: result.DETAIL.HIGH, LOW: result.DETAIL.LOW, PRICE: result.DETAIL.PRICE, VOLUME: result.DETAIL.VOLUME, LATEST_TRADING_DAY: result.DETAIL.LATEST_TRADING_DAY, PREVIOUS_CLOSE: result.DETAIL.PREVIOUS_CLOSE, CHANGE: result.DETAIL.CHANGE, CHANGE_PERCENT: result.DETAIL.CHANGE_PERCENT, }) } genTable(watchList) }) router.Run() }

This is the entry point of the executable program. In there, we declared an array watchList of struct Quote type to which stock data will be appended. Next, we created a channel with ch := make(chan Stock) to make the communication between main thread and child threads.

router := gin.Default(), creates gin router which is used to make a handler with router.GET("/stocks/", HandlerFunction(){})

3. Handlerfunction(c *gin.Context):
This handler takes the query parameter with stockSymbol := c.Query("symbol") using the Gin package and since the query might contain more than one string parameter separated by a comma, we derived an array of strings out of it with listOfSymbol := strings.Split(stockSymbol, ",").

Then for each parameter(stock symbol), we concurrently make GET request to the Alpha Vantage API with:

for _, symbol := range listOfSymbol{ go getStock(symbol, ch) }

Next for every GET request, Alpha Vantage sends back a response of stock data that go getStock(symbol, ch) as a child thread, passes through the channel to the main thread. In the main thread, we store stock data in the watchList array and generate a table with genTable(watchList).

4. Finally, we start the router using router.Run()

Our project directory should look like the project tree below:

.
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
├── main.go
└── stock.go

0 directories, 4 files

2. Dockerize Go App

In this part of the tutorial, we will dockerize the Go application newly built. To do so, stay in the working directory, create a file named Dockerfile where you put the code below:

FROM golang:1.18-alpine

WORKDIR /app

COPY go.mod ./
COPY go.sum ./

RUN go mod download


COPY *.go ./

RUN go build -o /stock-list

CMD [ "/stock-list" ]

FROM golang:1.18-alpine this line tells Docker to use goland:1.18-alpine as the base image of our Go app image. It comes with all tools and packages to compile and run our application.

WORKDIR /app this one will create a working direction inside the image that we are building.

COPY go.mod ./ and COPY go.sum ./ instructions copy go.mod and go.sum files to the working direction in our docker image /app.

RUN go mod download downloads our application dependencies inside the docker image working directory.

COPY *.go ./ this instruction copies every go file in the our Go app local machine directory to the working directory in the docker image.

RUN go build -o /stock-list this will compile our application and CMD [ "/stock-list" ] will execute our compiled code.

2.1 Build and Run docker image

After successfully creating the Dockerfile, let's build a docker image from it.

Note: Before doing that, make sure to have a Docker Hub account and with its credentials, sign in using docker login command in your local machine.

In the project directory /stock-list, use your Docker Hub ID in the command below to build the image:

$ docker build --tag <YOUR DOCKER HUB ID>/stock-list .

Visualize stock-list docker image by running this command:

Run the stock-list image:

$ docker run -p 8080:8080 <YOUR DOCKER HUB ID>/stock-list

In order to view our running image or container, let run the command below:


After running this command, we can see that the docker container of stock-list image is exposed to the 8080 on your local machine 0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp.

To see if the Go application is working correctly, we are going to make a GET request to the server using curl:

$ curl http://localhost:8080/stocks/?symbol=TSLA,TWTR,AAPL

Find more stock symbols here, to perform more test.

In addition, we can use different other commands to either stop the container in our case, the container running the stock-list image, restart or remove it.

To stop the container, let's take our container ID or container's name here;
Paste it to the command below:

$ docker stop <YOUR CONTAINER ID>

To restart, we can use the same container ID or container's name with this command:

$ docker restart <YOUR CONTAINER ID>

And to completely remove the container of stock-list image, still with the same container ID, let us use the following command:

$ docker rm <YOUR CONTAINER ID>

For more detailed docker commands, please check out the official documentation.

2.2 Deploy to Docker Hub

This is the last part of the tutorial where we will push our local stock-list image to Docker Hub. We successfully built our Go app and packed it with all necessary dependencies in a docker image; it is time to upload it to Docker Hub.

Let's push our image with this command:

$ docker push <YOUR DOCKER HUB ID>/stock-list


Doing this will make our stock-list image portable and accessible to other developers who can run it on their operating systems.

Our tutorial ends here guys, we built a Go application from scratch, we used some external go packages and made HTTP calls to a great API. To dockerize the application, we built a docker image from it, we ran it and pushed it to Docker Hub. Thanks for doing this together.

Find the project code here.
Items.Author: musolemasu

Items.Title: 一些反应。 | Some of the React.
Items.Description:
以下是我在制作项目时学到的一些概念和实践。

  1. 配料
  2. 和解
  3. 作品
  4. 反应路由器 v6
  5. 使用参考挂钩

在 React 中进行批处理。

当我开始使用useState时，我有一个误解，即一旦遇到setState语句就会渲染组件。 后来我意识到 React 不会在setState语句之后立即呈现。 任何状态更新操作都会一起批处理并排队等待在下一次渲染期间调用useState时进行计算。 组件仅在事件处理函数执行了它拥有的所有代码时才会呈现。 然后，在下一次渲染期间，计算那些批处理的状态更新，并将值返回给状态变量。 因此，当组件呈现并调用挂钩时，它们会将更新后的值返回给状态。 这是一个例子，

 export default function App () { const [ a , setA ] = useState ( 1 ); const handleBtnClick = () => { setA ( a + 1 ); setA ( a + 1 ); setA ( a + 1 ); setA ( a + 1 ); }; console . log ( " rendered " , a ); return ( < div className = " App " > < button onClick = { handleBtnClick } > do it < /button > < /div > ); }

如果你运行它，你会看到console.log将只运行一次并给出值2 。 如果你使用过React.StrictMode ，你可能会得到两次相同的输出。 现在，这个例子把我带到了下一点，即更新函数。

在这个例子中，因为我们在setA中提供了一个操作，所以setA被转换为setA(1+1)被转换为setA(2)然后这些更新被排队等待下一次渲染。 然后，在下一次渲染期间， useState有四个状态更新，并且都是setA(2) 。 为什么所有的都是 2？ 这是因为每个渲染都有自己的状态值，并且该状态值不会因特定渲染而改变。 您可能已经注意到，在调用useState(1)并且我们将数组解构为状态值和状态更新函数的行中，我们使用了const 。 这意味着我们不能在同一渲染期间更改状态值。 先前的渲染对于a的值为1 。 因此，所有的setA语句都被读取为setA(2) 。 返回给组件的最终值是2 。

在我们想要串行更新状态的情况下，与上面提到的情况不同，在每次更新时状态仅被相同的常量值2替换，我们将使用更新器函数。

更新程序函数是提供给setA的回调函数。 它的参数是此计算之前的最新状态值。 这是一个例子，

 const handleBtnClick = () => { setA (( a ) => a + 1 ); // this returns 2 setA (( a ) => a + 1 ); // this returns 3 setA (( a ) => a + 1 ); // this returns 4 setA (( a ) => a + 1 ); // this returns 5 };

通过提供回调函数，我们告诉 React 在下一次渲染期间计算状态值。

和解

这个过程从ReactDOM.render()方法开始。 这个render方法创建了一个虚拟 DOM。 但首先，介绍一下虚拟 DOM。

虚拟 DOM 是代表浏览器 DOM 的 JSON 对象。 与浏览器 DOM 相比，它非常快。 它是在每次状态更新时从头开始创建的。

为此，让我给你看这个例子，

 console . log ( createElement ( < p className = "App" > some random text </ p > ));

此代码给出以下输出，

对于要被称为不同的任何元素，这些属性中的任何一个都必须与先前虚拟 DOM 的相同元素的属性不同。

每当 React 看到不同的元素时，它不仅会重新创建该元素，还会重新创建它的所有子元素。 因此，拥有唯一键有助于 React 确定特定子节点是新的还是已更新或从子列表中删除。

在没有键的情况下，在列表顶部添加一个子项将意味着整个列表被销毁并重新创建。 但是拥有一个键会告诉 React 将新元素添加到列表中，而不是破坏整个列表。

还有一件事。 用 React Elements 来考虑整个组件树。 根组件将有children组件，其中将列出子组件，其中一些也会有children组件。 像这样继续下去，你可以想象一棵树正在形成，它从根开始，叶子节点是 HTML 元素。 这是 React 在 diffing 期间遍历以发现差异的组件树。 为了遍历这棵树，React 使用广度优先的方法。 为了说明深度优先遍历，假设在 diffing 期间，React 看到叶节点是不同的。 所以它会破坏这个节点并再次创建它。 现在，它转到父节点，发现这个节点也不同。 它破坏了这个父节点和它的子树并再次重新创建整个子树。 如果存在广度优先遍历，则可以在单个操作中完成相同的操作。 作品

组件可以以类似的方式组成。 高阶组件就是这样一个函数，它将子组件作为参数并返回包装在父组件中的组件。 作为参数传递的组件将根据用例而改变。 这是一个例子，

 const FeedPageWrapper = PostsSection => { const FeedPage = () => { return ( < div className = { `bg-off-secondary ${ styles . feedPageGrid } ` } > < div className = { ` ${ styles . navDiv } flx flx-maj-end` } > < NavBar /> </ div > < PostsSection /> { /*child component used here*/ } < div className = { styles . extraDiv } > { /* third div empty for now.*/ } </ div > </ div > ) } return FeedPage } export default FeedPageWrapper

在上面的示例中，我有一个高阶组件，它采用一个名为PostsSection的组件。 作为参数传递的这个组件将根据用户所在的页面而有所不同。 如果用户在书签页面上， PostsSection将有一个带有书签的帖子列表。 如果用户在用户提要页面上， PostsSection将有一个为用户个性化的帖子列表。

除了PostsSection之外，页面上的其他所有内容都相同。 因此，我决定在这里使用高阶组件。 这是该组件的使用方式，

 const BookmarksFeedPage = FeedPageWrapper ( BookmarksSection ) export default BookmarksFeedPage

我已将BookmarksSection作为参数传递，并且返回BookmarksFeedPage并在下一行中导出。 同样对于用户提要页面，

 const UserFeedPage = FeedPageWrapper ( UserFeedSection ) export default UserFeedPage

使用反应路由器的私有路由

私人路线是指为用户个性化的路线，只有在用户登录时才会显示。例如，在电子商务应用程序中，愿望清单和购物车页面是私人路线，因为它们会有不同的不同用户的数据，与产品页面不同。

这是算法，

  1. 检查当前路由是否是私有的。
  2. 如果是，则检查用户是否已登录。
  3. 如果用户已登录，则让用户继续使用此路由。
  4. 如果用户未登录，则将用户重定向到登录页面。
  5. 如果当前路由不是私有的，那么让用户继续使用这条路由。
 < Route path = { ROUTE_CART } element = { < RequireAuth > < Cart /> </ RequireAuth > } />

在上面的代码中，我将<Cart/>包裹在<RequireAuth/>中，用于检查用户是否登录。



原文:

Here are the some of the concepts and practices that I learnt while making my projects.

  1. Batching
  2. Reconciliation
  3. Composition
  4. React Router v6
  5. CSS Modules
  6. useRef Hook

Batching in React.

When I started using useState, I had a misconception that the component is rendered as soon as the setState statement is encountered. I realized this later that React does not render right after the setState statement. Any state update operations are batched together and queued to be computed when the useState is invoked during the next render. The component renders only when the event handler function has executed all the code that it had. Then, during the next render, those state updates that were batched are computed and the value is returned to the state variable. Thus, when the component renders and the hooks are invoked, they return the updated value to the state. Here is an example,

export default function App() { const [a, setA] = useState(1); const handleBtnClick = () => { setA(a + 1); setA(a + 1); setA(a + 1); setA(a + 1); }; console.log("rendered", a); return ( <div className="App"> <button onClick={handleBtnClick}>do it</button>
 </div>
 );
}

If you run this, you will see that the console.log will run only once and give the value 2. If you have used React.StrictMode you might get the same output twice. Now, this example brings me to the next point which is the updater function.

In this example, since we are providing an operation inside setA, the setA is converted to setA(1+1) which is converted to setA(2) and then these updates are queued for the next render. Then, during the next render, useState has four state updates and all of them are setA(2). Why 2 for all of them? That is because every render has it's own state value and this state value does not change for that particular render. You might have noticed that in the line where useState(1) is invoked and we are de-structuring the array into the state value and state update function, we have used const. This means that we cannot change the state value during the same render. The previous render had the value of 1 for a. Therefore, all the setA statements were read as setA(2). The final value that is returned to the component is 2.

In case of a scenario where we want to serially update the state unlike the scenario mentioned above where the state was only replaced by the same constant value of 2 on every update, we would use an updater function.

An updater function is a callback function that is supplied to the setA. It's argument is the latest state value prior to this calculation. Here is an example,

const handleBtnClick = () => { setA((a) => a + 1); // this returns 2 setA((a) => a + 1); // this returns 3 setA((a) => a + 1); // this returns 4 setA((a) => a + 1); // this returns 5 };

By giving a callback function, we are telling React to calculate the state value during the next render.

Reconciliation

React uses this algorithm to make sure the DOM rendering is as efficient as possible. React has the diffing algorithm through which it narrows down which elements are different so that only those elements are updated in the browser DOM. This process starts with ReactDOM.render() method. This render method creates a virtual DOM. During diffing, the newly created virtual DOM is compared with the virtual DOM before the state update. But first, a bit about virtual DOM.

Virtual DOM is a JSON object that represents the browser DOM. It is extremely fast compared to the browser DOM. It is created from scratch on every state update.

How does React compare the corresponding elements from two different virtual DOMs? For that, let me show you this example,

console.log(
createElement(
<p className="App">some random text</p>
));

This code gives the following output,

React sees every node like this and then compares their respective properties. For any element to be called different, any of these properties have to differ from the properties of the same element of the previous virtual DOM.

All the child nodes are mentioned in children object. React gives warning for having unique keys for child nodes. Whenever React sees a different element, it not only re-creates that element but also all its children. So, having a unique key helps React in determining whether a particular child node is new or updated or removed from the list of children.

In a case without keys, adding a child on top of the list would mean that the whole list is destroyed and re-created. But having a key would tell the React to add the new element to the list instead of destroying the whole list.

One more thing. Think of the whole component tree in terms of a React Elements. The root component would have children where the child components would be listed and some of these would have children too. Continuing like this you can imagine a tree is forming that starts at the root and the leaf nodes are the HTML elements. This is the component tree that React is traversing during the diffing to spot the differences. To traverse this tree, React uses breadth first approach. To make a case for depth first traversal, let's say during diffing, React sees that a leaf node is different. So it destroys this node and creates it again. Now, it goes to the parent node and sees that this node is also different. It destroys this parent node and it's subtree and re-creates the whole subtree again. The same could have been done in a single operation had there been a breadth first traversal. React would first check the parent node instead of going directly to the child node.

Once the process of diffing is complete, React prepares a list of minimum updates required to be done on the browser DOM.

Composition

React uses the idea of function composition from JS. Components can be composed in a similar manner. Higher order component is one such function that takes the child component as an argument and returns this component wrapped in the parent component. What component is passed as an argument will change depending on the use case. Here is an example,

const FeedPageWrapper = PostsSection => { const FeedPage = () => { return ( <div className={`bg-off-secondary ${styles.feedPageGrid}`}> <div className={`${styles.navDiv} flx flx-maj-end`}> <NavBar /> </div> <PostsSection /> {/*child component used here*/} <div className={styles.extraDiv}> {/* third div empty for now.*/} </div> </div> ) } return FeedPage
} export default FeedPageWrapper

In the above example, I have a higher order component that takes a component called PostsSection. This component that is passed as an argument will differ based on the page that the user is on. If the user is on bookmarks page, PostsSection will have a list of bookmarked posts. If the user is on user-feed page, PostsSection will have a list of posts personalized for the user.

Apart from the PostsSection, everything else on the page will be the same. Hence, I decided to use the higher order component here. Here is how this component will be used,

const BookmarksFeedPage = FeedPageWrapper(BookmarksSection) export default BookmarksFeedPage

I have passed BookmarksSection as the argument and BookmarksFeedPage is returned that is exported in the next line. Similarly for the user-feed page,

const UserFeedPage = FeedPageWrapper(UserFeedSection) export default UserFeedPage

Private routes using react router

By private routes I mean the routes that are personalized for the users and should only be shown if a user is logged in. For example, in an e-commerce app, wish-list and cart pages are the private routes because they will have different data for different users, unlike the products page.

Here is the algorithm,

  1. Check if the current route is private or not.
  2. If it is, then check whether the user is logged in or not.
  3. If user is logged in, let the user continue with this route.
  4. If user is not logged in, re-direct the user to the login page.
  5. If the current route is not private, then let the user continue with this route.   
<Route path={ROUTE_CART} element={ <RequireAuth> <Cart /> </RequireAuth>
} />

In the above code, I have wrapped <Cart/> inside <RequireAuth/> which checks whether the user is logged in or not.

Items.Title: Apache APISIX 插件编排概述 | Apache APISIX Plugins Orchestration Overview
Items.Description:
目前， Apache APISIX API Gateway 提供了 50 多个内置插件，分为几类，以满足常见的使用场景。 它涵盖了身份验证、安全性、流量控制、无服务器、可观察性等等。

问题 1

插件无法满足企业的所有需求，因为每个公司都有一些独特的需求。 所以，我们经常需要对现有的插件做一些自定义开发来满足我们的业务需求。 这是一个实际问题，因为插件开发仍然需要更多技能，例如特定编程语言。 对于插件开发，不同的开源项目有不同的解决方案。

问题 2

有时您的服务需要您一起使用许多插件。 现在的问题是插件不共享上下文，并且无法在迄今为止已经开源的所有其他 API 网关中进行协作。

插件编排

在接下来的视频教程中，您将了解低代码的 Apache APISIX 插件编排能力，如何解决上述问题，并帮助企业将新 API Gateway 插件的开发成本降低到几乎为零。 我们可以在 UI 仪表板上以“拖放”的方式轻松编排所有现有插件。 您还可以通过使用条件组件来组合不同的插件来创建新的“插件”。

插件编排在行动

在观看视频教程的过程中，演示了我们根据之前插件的处理结果来决定后续 API 请求处理逻辑的场景。

  • 我们的 API 仅接收来自具有ip-restriction插件控制的白名单 IP 的请求。
  • 如果IP在白名单中， limit-count接管并限制60秒时间窗口内的请求数2。
  • 如果新请求来自不同的 IP 范围，插件会返回 403（请求的资源被禁止）HTTP 状态码。

将所有构建块（插件，条件）放入绘图板后，我们将有一个类似于下图的图表：

想了解更多⤵️

➔ Read Overview of Apache APISIX API Gateway Plugins ➔ 阅读Apache APISIX API 网关插件概述

➔ Read API Observability with Apache APISIX Plugins ➔使用 Apache APISIX 插件读取 API 可观察性

➔ Read Centralized Authentication with Apache APISIX Plugins ➔使用 Apache APISIX 插件读取集中式身份验证

➔ Watch Video Tutorial Getting Started with Apache APISIX ➔ 观看视频教程Apache APISIX 入门

➔ Watch Video Tutorial Getting Started with Apache APISIX Dashboard ➔ 观看视频教程Apache APISIX 仪表板入门



原文:

Currently, Apache APISIX API Gateway provides more than 50 built-in plugins broken down into several categories to meet the common usage scenarios. It covers authentication, security, traffic control, serverless, observability, and many more.

Problem 1

Plugins are unable to meet all the needs of the enterprise because each company has some unique needs. So, we often need to do some custom development of existing plugins to meet our business requirements. This is an actual problem because plugin development still requires more skills like in the specific programming language. For plugin development, different open-source projects have different solutions.

Problem 2

Sometimes your service requires you to use many plugins together. The problem now is that the plug-ins do not share context and cannot cooperate in all other API gateways that have been open source so far.

Plugins Orchestration

In the following video tutorial, you will learn with low-code Apache APISIX plugin orchestration capability, how the above-mentioned problems can be solved and can help businesses to reduce the development cost of the new API Gateway Plugin to almost zero. We can easily orchestrate all existing plugins in a “drag-and-drop” way on the UI dashboard. You can also combine different plugins by using conditional components to create new “plugins”.

Plugin Orchestration in action

As you watch the video tutorial, the scenario was demonstrated where we decide the subsequent API request processing logic based on the processing result of previous plugin.

  • Our API receives requests only from whitelisted IPs with the ip-restriction plugin control.
  • If the IP is in the whitelist, limit-count takes over and limits the number of requests 2 within 60 seconds time window.
  • If new request comes from different IP range, the plugin returns a 403 (requested resource is forbidden) HTTP status code.

After putting all building blocks to the drawing board (plugins, condition), we will have a diagram similar to below:

To learn more⤵️

➔ Read API Observability with Apache APISIX Plugins

➔ Read Centralized Authentication with Apache APISIX Plugins

➔ Watch Video Tutorial Getting Started with Apache APISIX

➔ Watch Video Tutorial Getting Started with Apache APISIX Dashboard

Items.Title: 它是什么？ 它是如何工作的？ 为什么要使用它？ | Destructuring in JavaScript. What is it? How does it work? Why should you use it?
Items.Description:
它可用于使您的代码更具可读性和简洁性。

在这篇博文中，我们将了解什么是解构，它是如何工作的，以及使用它的一些好处。

解构如何运作？

解构很简单，尽管乍一看似乎很神奇且令人困惑。 它通过获取数组或对象并从其属性创建变量来工作。

解构数组

例如，假设您有一个名为myNumbers的数字数组：

 const myNumbers = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ];

您可以使用解构从数组元素创建变量。

 const [ first , second , third , fourth , fifth ] = myNumbers ; console . log ( first ); // 1 console . log ( second ); // 2 console . log ( third ); // 3 console . log ( fourth ); // 4 console . log ( fifth ); // 5

如您所见，解构使您可以从数组中提取数据并将其分配给变量。

解构对象

您还可以使用解构从对象中提取数据。

例如，假设您有一个具有 name、age 和 country 属性的person对象：

 const person = { name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' };

您可以使用解构从对象的属性中创建变量。

 const { name , age , country } = person ; console . log ( name ); // John Doe console . log ( age ); // 30 console . log ( country ); // United States

如上所述，我们可以使用解构来提取对象属性并将它们分配给变量。

解构嵌套数据结构

解构也可用于从嵌套数据结构中提取数据。

假设您有一组具有名称、年龄和国家/地区属性的对象。

 const people = [ { name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' }, { name : ' Jane Doe ' , age : 20 , country : ' Canada ' } ];

您可以使用解构从嵌套对象的属性中创建变量。

 const [{ name : name1 , age : age1 , country : country1 }, { name : name2 , age : age2 , country : country2 }] = people ; console . log ( name1 ); // John Doe console . log ( age1 ); // 30 console . log ( country1 ); // United States console . log ( name2 ); // Jane Doe console . log ( age2 ); // 20 console . log ( country2 ); // Canada

这有助于我们简化更复杂的数据结构并从数组的每个元素中提取值。

解构函数参数

解构也可用于从函数参数中提取数据。

假设我们有一个函数接受一个具有 name、age 和 country 属性的对象：

 function sayHello ({ name , age , country }) { console . log ( `Hello, my name is ${ name } . I'm ${ age } years old and I'm from ${ country } .` ); }

您可以使用解构从对象中提取数据并将其作为参数传递给函数。

 sayHello ({ name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' }); // Hello, my name is John Doe. I'm 30 years old and I'm from United States.

如您所见，解构可以轻松地从数据结构中提取数据并将其作为参数传递给函数。

使用解构的好处

解构可用于使您的代码更具可读性和简洁性。 为了展示它，我们将比较使用解构的代码和不使用我们上面编写的示例的代码。

数组比较

让我们使用myNumbers数组并尝试将其中的所有整数相加。

如果不进行解构，您将不得不编写以下代码：

 const myNumbers = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ]; let sum = 0 ; for ( const num of myNumbers ) { sum += num ; } console . log ( sum ); // 15

通过解构，您可以编写以下代码：

 const myNumbers = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ]; const [ first , second , third , fourth , fifth ] = myNumbers ; const sum = first + second + third + fourth + fifth ; console . log ( sum ); // 15

如您所见，解构可以使您的代码更简洁。

让我们看一下对象示例以及重组如何帮助我们使代码更具可读性。

对象比较

我们将使用前面示例中的person对象。

如果不进行解构，您将不得不编写以下代码：

 const person = { name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' }; console . log ( person . name ); // John Doe console . log ( person . age ); // 30 console . log ( person . country ); // United States

通过解构，您可以编写以下代码：

 const person = { name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' }; const { name , age , country } = person ; console . log ( name ); // John Doe console . log ( age ); // 30 console . log ( country ); // United States

使用解构使我们的代码更具可读性。

提取函数参数比较

最后但同样重要的是，让我们看看如何使用解构来提取函数参数可以帮助我们更容易理解和使用：

我们将使用上面示例中已经熟悉的sayHello函数。

如果不进行解构，您将不得不编写以下代码：

 function sayHello ( person ) { console . log ( `Hello, my name is ${ person . name } . I'm ${ person . age } years old and I'm from ${ person . country } .` ); } sayHello ({ name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' });

通过解构，您可以编写以下代码：

 function sayHello ({ name , age , country }) { console . log ( `Hello, my name is ${ name } . I'm ${ age } years old and I'm from ${ country } .` ); } sayHello ({ name : ' John Doe ' , age : 30 , country : ' United States ' });

我们的解构示例更加简洁易读。

结论

解构是一项强大的功能，可用于使您的代码更具可读性和简洁性。 这是一种从数据结构中提取数据的便捷方式，无需编写大量代码。

如果你还没有在你的 JavaScript 代码中使用解构，我鼓励你试一试——它值得添加到你的工具箱中。



原文:

Destructuring in JavaScript is a powerful feature that lets you extract values from data structures like arrays and objects. It can be used to make your code more readable and concise.

In this blog post, we'll take a look at what destructuring is, how it works, and some of the benefits of using it.

How does destructuring work?

Destructuring is simple though it might seem magical and confusing at first glance. It works by taking an array or object and creating variables from its properties.

Destructuring arrays

For example, let's say you have an array of numbers called myNumbers:

const myNumbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

You can use destructuring to create variables from the elements of the array.

const [first, second, third, fourth, fifth] = myNumbers; console.log(first); // 1
console.log(second); // 2
console.log(third); // 3
console.log(fourth); // 4
console.log(fifth); // 5

As you can see, destructuring lets you extract data from arrays and assign it to variables.

Destructuring objects

You can also use destructuring to extract data from objects.

For example, let's say you have a person object with properties name, age, and country:

const person = { name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States '
};

You can use destructuring to create variables from the object's properties.

const { name, age, country } = person; console.log(name); // John Doe
console.log(age); // 30
console.log(country); // United States

As presented above we can use destructuring to extract object properties and assign them to the variables.

Destructuring nested data structures

Destructuring can also be used to extract data from nested data structures.

Let's say you have an array of objects with properties name, age, and country.

const people = [ { name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States ' }, { name: ' Jane Doe ', age: 20, country: ' Canada ' }
];

You can use destructuring to create variables from the nested object's properties.

const [{ name: name1, age: age1, country: country1 }, { name: name2, age: age2, country: country2 }] = people; console.log(name1); // John Doe
console.log(age1); // 30
console.log(country1); // United States console.log(name2); // Jane Doe
console.log(age2); // 20
console.log(country2); // Canada

This helps us simplify more complex data structure and extract values form each element of array.

Destructuring function arguments

Destructuring can also be used to extract data from function arguments.

Given that we have a function that takes an object with properties name, age, and country:

function sayHello({ name, age, country }) { console.log(`Hello, my name is ${name}. I'm ${age} years old and I'm from ${country}.`);
}

You can use destructuring to extract the data from the object and pass it as an argument to the function.

sayHello({ name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States '
}); // Hello, my name is John Doe. I'm 30 years old and I'm from United States.

As you can see, destructuring makes it easy to extract data from data structures and pass it to functions as arguments.

Benefits of Using Destructuring

Destructuring can be used to make your code more readable and concise. To show it we will compare code that uses destructuring with one that doesn't for the examples we wrote above.

Array comparison

Let's take our myNumbers array and try to sum up all the integers inside it.

Without destructuring, you would have to write the following code:

const myNumbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; let sum = 0; for (const num of myNumbers) { sum += num;
} console.log(sum); // 15

With destructuring, you can write the following code:

const myNumbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const [first, second, third, fourth, fifth] = myNumbers; const sum = first + second + third + fourth + fifth; console.log(sum); // 15

As you can see, destructuring can make your code more concise.

Let's take a look at object examples and how restructuring can help us make code more readable.

Object comparison

We will use our person object from the previous example.

Without destructuring, you would have to write the following code:

const person = { name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States '
}; console.log(person.name); // John Doe
console.log(person.age); // 30
console.log(person.country); // United States

With destructuring, you can write the following code:

const person = { name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States '
}; const { name, age, country } = person; console.log(name); // John Doe
console.log(age); // 30
console.log(country); // United States

Using destructuring makes our code more readable.

Extracting function arguments comparison

Last but not least let's see how using destructuring to extract function arguments can help us make it easier to understand and work with:

We will use the sayHello function we are already familiar with from the example above.

Without destructuring, you would have to write the following code:

function sayHello(person) { console.log(`Hello, my name is ${person.name}. I'm ${person.age} years old and I'm from ${person.country}.`);
} sayHello({ name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States '
});

With destructuring, you can write the following code:

function sayHello({ name, age, country }) { console.log(`Hello, my name is ${name}. I'm ${age} years old and I'm from ${country}.`);
} sayHello({ name: ' John Doe ', age: 30, country: ' United States '
});

Our destructured example is both more concise and readable.

Conclusion

Destructuring is a powerful feature that can be used to make your code more readable and concise. It is a convenient way to extract data from data structures without having to write a lot of code.

If you're not already using destructuring in your JavaScript code, I encourage you to give it a try - it is worth adding to your toolbox.

}

Items.Title: React Native Absolute Imports 快速简单！ | React Native Absolute Imports Quick & Easy!
Items.Description:
安丽莎·马哈詹

发表于绝对导入有助于简化路径并随着项目的增长更好地组织项目。 此外，使用绝对导入更容易将导入的代码复制粘贴到项目中的另一个文件中，而不必修改导入路径。 当项目的文件夹结构复杂时，我们会面临项目中存在较长的相对导入，如下所示：

import Input from '../../../components/form/input';

它可能很难重构并且看起来很乱。 解决方案是将相对导入转换为绝对导入。

第 1 步 — 安装babel-plugin-module-resolver插件

$ npm install --save-dev babel-plugin-module-resolver

或者

$ yarn add --dev babel-plugin-module-resolver

第 2 步 — 更新babel.config.js
在babel.config.js中添加以下代码片段

module.exports = { plugins: [ [ 'module-resolver', { alias: { '@app': './src', }, }, ], ], }

注意： @app是一个别名，可以给任何人想要的任何东西。

第 3 步 — 设置jsconfig.json或tsconfig.json

使用 Javascript
创建/打开jsconfig.json文件（在项目的根目录中）并在compilerOptions中添加baseUrl和paths设置，如下面的片段所示：

 { "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": ".", "paths" : { "@app/*": ["src/*"] } } }

使用打字稿
如果您在 react native 项目中使用 TypeScript，请更新tsconfig.json文件（在项目的根目录中）并在compilerOptions中添加与 Javascript 相同的设置。

 { "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": ".", "paths" : { "@app/*": ["src/*"] } } }

第 4 步 — 实施绝对导入
绝对导入设置现在已成功配置src文件夹作为自定义基本目录，我们可以导入位于src/components/form/input.js的输入组件，如下所示：

import Input from '@app/components/form/input';

就是这样！ 快速简单！ 快乐学习！

塞巴斯蒂安·洛伯—— Apr 20 4月20日



原文:

Anlisha Maharjan

Posted on May 10

Absolute imports help to simplify the paths and better organize project as it grows. Also with absolute imports its easier to copy-paste the code with imports into another file in the project and not have to tinker with import paths. 😆

When project’s folder structure is complex, we are facing to have long relative imports in the project like this:

import Input from ‘../../../components/form/input’;

It can be pretty hard to refactor and looks messy. The solution is to convert relative imports to absolute imports.

Step 1 — Install babel-plugin-module-resolver plugin

$ npm install --save-dev babel-plugin-module-resolver

Or

$ yarn add --dev babel-plugin-module-resolver

Step 2 — Update babel.config.js
Add the following code snippet in babel.config.js

module.exports = {
  plugins: [
    [
      'module-resolver',
      {
        alias: {
          '@app': './src',
        },
      },
    ],
  ],
}

Note: @app is an alias one can give whatever one want.

Step 3 — Setup jsconfig.json or tsconfig.json

Using Javascript
Create/Open jsconfig.json file (in the root directory of the project) and add baseUrl and paths setting inside compilerOptions as shown in snippet below:

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths" : {
      "@app/*": ["src/*"]
    }
  }
}

Using TypeScript
If you’re using TypeScript in react native project, update tsconfig.json file (in the root directory of the project) and add the same setting inside compilerOptions as Javascript.

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths" : {
      "@app/*": ["src/*"]
    }
  }
}

Step 4 — Implement Absolute Import
Absolute imports setting is now successfully configured with src folder as custom base directory and we can import a input component located at src/components/form/input.js from like so:

import Input from '@app/components/form/input';

That's it! Quick & Easy! Happy Learning!

Sebastien Lorber - Apr 20

Items.Title: 从音乐到代码 | From Music to Code
Items.Description:
发表于介绍：
各位互联网朋友您好！ 这篇文章将成为我希望成为我作为博主的旅程的起点。 我想从自我介绍开始这个系列。

第一章：高中
你好！ 我叫安德鲁，今年 28 岁。 自学成才的老 Web 开发人员。 通常我不会包括我的年龄，但我认为这与上下文相关。 正如标题所述，Web 开发并不是我追求的第一条道路。 在我 16 岁的时候，我基本上只关心三件事：小号、吉他和电子游戏。 我练习了小号......很多！ 通过这种做法，我能够成功地在 2010 和 2011 赛季试镜我们的密西西比雄狮全州乐队。 正是这个团体让我通过音乐表演的机会体验了这个世界。 我最喜欢的地方之一是澳大利亚的悉尼。

你还记得我之前提到过“我基本上关心三件事”吗？ 好成绩不包括在该列表中。 在学术方面，我是一个非常糟糕的高中生，这让我的 GPA 很差。 我没有为 ACT 学习，这导致​​了低于标准的分数。 我在密西西比州立大学试镜，希望能获得体面的奖学金，但这并没有发生。 听从姐姐的建议，我决定去海因兹社区学院申请和试听。 我不确定你，读者，是否相信虽然我们希望的事情没有发生，但这并不意味着伟大的事情不是指日可待，但这正是发生的事情。

第二章：海因兹社区学院（美好的岁月）
我在一个人们，尤其是我的同龄人，看不起社区学院的地方长大。 如果你没有上过某某大学，那么你自然会被视为低人一等。 就我个人而言，就读海因兹社区学院是迄今为止我一生中最好的经历之一。 我的老师们不仅在他们的工作中表现出色，他们对学生也非常关心和同情。 在此期间，我参加了五个音乐合奏团，包括 The Hinds Community College Marching Band、Wind Ensemble、Rock Band 101、Jazz Band 和 Brass Quintet。

我的 2012 年春季学期，我们的摇滚乐队 101 和铜管五重奏组被邀请与布里奇沃特和汤顿学院的乐队合作，这意味着是时候前往英国了！ 好像英格兰一年还不够兴奋，那个夏天我和世界级的鼓和号角军团一起巡演了 2012 年和 2013 年国际鼓队赛季的幻影军团。 与这个小组一起表演是我迄今为止一生中最困难和最有意义的经历之一。 每个季节，我们旅行了大约。 我们的睡眠安排包括在包车上睡几个小时，或者更糟糕的是......在健身房地板上。 我记得有一次在我们 2012 赛季刚开始的时候，我们的公共汽车抛锚了，我们不得不挤在另外两辆公共汽车上，行驶了数百英里。 我不得不坐在公共汽车的地板上，膝盖靠在胸前，因为我前面和后面的人都在做同样的事情。 再说一次，如果你曾经参加过游行或看过 DCI 表演，那么表演期间发生的魔力弥补了所有……其他的东西。

如果你跟上，自 2010 年以来每年都会发生某种音乐冒险。2014 年 5 月，Hinds Wind Ensemble、爵士乐队和铜管五重奏受邀在法国巴黎的不同地点演出。 我们的第一次演出是在诺曼底美军公墓，在那里我有机会在 70 周年献花仪式上表演 Taps。 我们的管乐团和铜管五重奏也有机会表演。 我们演奏的最令人难忘的作品之一是 John Williams 的“Hymn to the Fallen”。 为了提供一些关于我们周围环境的背景信息，如果你从我们表演的地方看公墓，那么右边是一个悬崖，可以俯瞰奥马哈海滩，那里是诺曼底登陆日之一。 能够看到我们历史中如此可怕的部分发生在哪里的经历是超现实的。 我们表演的其他场地包括各种教堂和卢森堡花园。

第 3 章：过度播放您最喜欢的歌曲
由于各种原因，我决定将我在 Hinds CC 的逗留时间延长 4 年，我不会在这里讨论。 如果我在上一章中没有说明，我在 Hinds 的头两年简直是惊人的。 然而，就像我们都可能经历过我们最喜欢的歌曲成为我们想要听的最后一件事的那一刻，因为我们在一段荒谬的时间内重播了它，我想我在 Hinds 的时间过长了。 你看，当你有某些表演机会时，例如在英国或法国表演时，某些烦恼变得更容易被忽视。 海因兹不是一所音乐学院或一些著名的音乐学校，每个人都有令人难以置信的职业道德，我开始对我的许多同龄人产生一种不公平的怨恨。 我不知道如何写这篇文章而不显得傲慢或自负，但是当你与有意识的专注和决心如此一致的团体一起表演时，少经历比任何事情都更令人沮丧。 我知道这不公平，因为我是选择留下来的人，但那是我当时的现实。 我的乐队总监甚至警告我，如果我决定留任第四年，我会很痛苦。 我最终休掉了 2015 年秋季学期的假，试图弄清楚我的下一步行动……

第 4 章：Delta State University我的记忆有些模糊，但我想是 2015 年夏天，我和当时的小号老师 Robert Cheesman 一起参加了国际小号公会会议。 这是一次令人难以置信的体验，我们听到了许多小号独奏家和合奏。 正是在这里，奇斯曼先生把我介绍给了迈克尔·埃尔泽博士。 快进几个月，切斯曼先生告诉我，我应该认真考虑参加三角洲州立大学的试镜（这是 Ellzey 博士最近开始教学的地方）。 我当然接受了他的建议，因为在我在 Hinds CC 期间，他已成为我最大的导师之一。 我最终参加了试镜，并最终在 2016 年春季学期开始了我在 DSU 的冒险。 请记住，我考虑上这所学校只是因为一个人，埃尔泽博士。 如果您，读者，恰好是您申请大学的年龄，请尽职调查并研究您可能与该机构有任何关系的各个方面。 我没有这样做。 相反，我在 Hinds CC 开始产生的同样的怨恨只会越来越强烈。 我不确定你是否熟悉密西西比三角洲，但那里几乎……什么都没有……。

这也包括任何音乐机会。 我到了学位课程的最后阶段，我不得不在大学从批准的名单中选择的初中/高中进行一个学期的学生教学，但是一旦我在毕业时走过那个阶段，我就断绝了与几乎完全是音乐。 那是在2019年。

第 5 章：从音乐到代码
当我毕业并开始从音乐过渡时，我开始经历一波不确定的浪潮，就像海啸一样，会冲进来，然后退去，就在事情开始变得清晰时，浪潮又会回来。 音乐已经成为我身份的重要组成部分，没有它，我真的不知道我还“擅长”什么。 出于个人原因，我搬到了阿肯色州的一个小镇，那里比克利夫兰还大，但对于那些希望在互联网之外实现远大梦想的 20 多岁的人来说，仍然没有任何真正的机会。 我在这里的第一份工作是飞行员旅行中心的一名助理，然后很快转为星巴克的值班主管。 我最好的朋友之一在 2020 年 2 月创办了自己的科技创业公司，并让我加入了她的社交媒体营销团队。 在很短的时间内，这似乎是一些可能“坚持”的伟大事物的开始。 我们当时的 CTO 听说我对编码很感兴趣。 他向我介绍了 Brad Traversy 关于 Bootstrap 主题的 Udemy 课程。 我很喜欢它。 我慢慢地觉得我开始找到我的下一个“东西”了！ 显然，我是一个完整的初学者，所以我有自己的局限性。 我的任务是做一些较小的工作，例如设计和开发一个专门用于我们常见问题解答的页面，以便我可以建立在先验知识的基础上。

由于我不能透露的原因，该公司与我们的 CTO 分开了，我很快发现自己不得不自己提出问题并找到自己的答案。 我开始发现其他 YouTube 主播，例如 DevelopedbyEd、The Net Ninja、Lama Dev、Kevin Powell、JavaScript Mastery 以及许多其他让花时间学习编码变得不那么繁琐而获得更多回报的人。 我可能会写一篇关于我在学习音乐和学习代码之间发现的相似之处的帖子，但现在，重要的是要知道，当你学习音乐时，你有你的教授，你每周会见一次来学习并完善您的乐器、练习曲和其他曲目的基础知识。 几乎不可能偏离“教学大纲”，对于像我这样的人来说，这是因祸得福，因为一旦我接受了自己学习代码的挑战，我发现我只想在一次。 我希望我有一个教学大纲来让我走上正轨。 回顾过去两年，我觉得自己浪费了很多时间试图自己弄清楚某些事情，或者在我的编码之旅的这一点上哪个“堆栈”最适合我。 我要说的是，虽然我的想法可能是消极的，但我到达现在的位置所感受到的满足感似乎是值得的。 虽然我仍然有几天或几周的时间，动力似乎非常陌生，但我对自己的能力变得更加自在和自信。 我还必须提醒自己，我学习音乐已经超过 12 年了，而我只写了 2 年的代码。 老实说，如果你只计算我实际练习代码的连续天数，那将远远少于 2 年。 这几乎让我们走到了现在！ 从这里开始，我想开始定期创建针对代码的较短帖子。 有一百万种不同的方式来解释概念和基本原理，所以我将尝试创造而不是复制。 如果你坚持到了最后，那我向你致敬！

结论：
我知道这篇文章的大部分内容都围绕着我的生活和音乐而不是代码，但如果我试图以其他方式讲述它，那将是一个虚构的故事。 我也认识到，这篇文章可能永远不会被另一双眼睛看到，但如果事实证明这是错误的，并且您不确定当前人生道路的稳定性，请知道您并不孤单。 我总是乐于交谈:)

直到下一次！

安德鲁·T·拉德
Creator of ALStudio ALStudio 创始人



原文:

alstudio93

Posted on May 10

Introduction:
Hello friends of the internet! This post will become what I hope to be the starting point to my journey as a blogger. I would like to start off this series by introducing myself.

Chapter One: High School
Hi! My name is Andrew, and I am a 28yr. old self-taught Web Developer. Normally I wouldn't include my age, but I think it is relevant for the sake of context. As the title states, Web Development was not the first path that I pursued. When I was 16, I essentially cared about 3 things: trumpet, guitar, and video games. I practiced the trumpet... a lot! Through that practice, I was able to successfully audition into our Mississippi Lions All-State Band for the 2010 and 2011 seasons. It was this group that allowed me to experience the world through musical performance opportunities. One of my favorite locations was Sydney, Australia.

Do you recall earlier when I mentioned that, "I essentially cared about 3 things"? Well grades were not included in that list. I was... a pretty terrible high school student when it came to academics, which landed me a poor GPA. I didn't study for the ACT, which resulted in a sub-par score. I auditioned at Mississippi State University in hopes of getting a decent scholarship, but that didn't happen. Taking the advice of my sister, I decided to apply and audition at Hinds Community College. I'm not sure if you, the reader, believes in the idea that although something we hoped for didn't happen, it doesn't mean that something great isn't just around the corner, but that is exactly what happened.

Chapter Two: Hinds Community College (The Good Years)
I grew up in a place where people, especially my peers, looked down on Community Colleges. If you didn't attend such and such University, then you were automatically looked at as inferior. For me personally, attending Hinds Community College was one of the best experiences of my life thus far. My teachers were not only amazing at their job, they had immense care and compassion for their students. During my time there, I performed in five musical ensembles which consisted of The Hinds Community College Marching Band, Wind Ensemble, Rock Band 101, Jazz Band, and the Brass Quintet.

Hinds CC also had an "International Study in England" program with Bridgwater & Taunton College in Somerset, England. My Spring semester of 2012, our Rock Band 101 and Brass Quintet groups were invited to collaborate with the groups from Bridgwater & Taunton College, which meant it was time to head to England! As if England wasn’t enough excitement for one year, that summer I toured with the World Class Drum and Bugle Corps, Phantom Regiment for the 2012 and 2013 Drum Corps International seasons. Performing with this group was one of the most difficult and rewarding experiences of my life to date. Each season, we traveled approx. 50,000 miles throughout the United States performing in various venues ranging from highschool to NFL stadiums. Our sleeping arrangements consisted of catching a couple of hours of sleep on the charter bus, or even worse.. on a gym floor. I remember there was one time at the very beginning of our 2012 season where our bus broke down and we had to cram onto the other two buses for what seemed like hundreds of miles. I had to sit on the floor of the bus with my knees to my chest because the people in front and behind me were doing the same thing. Again, if you've ever marched or have seen a DCI show, then the magic that happens during the performances makes up for all the... other stuff.

If you're keeping up, some sort of musical venture has happened every year since 2010. In May of 2014, the Hinds Wind Ensemble, Jazz Band, and Brass Quintet were invited to perform in various locations in Paris, France. Our first performance was at the Normandy American Cemetery where I was given the opportunity to perform Taps during the 70th Anniversary Wreath Laying Ceremony. Our Wind Ensemble and Brass Quintet were also given the opportunity to perform. One of the most memorable pieces we performed was "Hymn to the Fallen" by John Williams. To provide some context for what our surroundings were like, if you were to look at the Cemetery from where we performed, then directly to the right was a cliff that overlooked Omaha Beach which is where one of the D-Day landings took place. The experience of being able to see where such a horrific part of our history took place was surreal. Other venues we performed at included various churches and Luxembourg Gardens.

2014 was the last time I traveled as a member of a performance ensemble and now that I look back on it, I think that is where things began to change.

Chapter 3: Overplaying your Favorite Song
I decided to extend my stay at Hinds CC for a 4th year for various reasons which I will not get into here. If I didn't make it obvious in the previous chapter, my first two years at Hinds were nothing short of amazing. However, just like we've all likely experienced the moment when our favorite song becomes the last thing we want to listen to because we had it on replay for some ridiculous amount of time, I think I overextended my time at Hinds. You see, when you have certain performance opportunities such as performing in England or France, certain annoyances become easier to overlook. Hinds was not a conservatory or some prestigious school of music where everybody had incredible work ethics, and I began to develop an unfair resentment towards many of my peers. I don’t know how to write this without sounding arrogant or conceited, but when you have performed with groups where the level of intentional focus and determination was so consistent, to experience anything less is more demoralizing than anything. I know it wasn't fair because I was the one that chose to stick around, but that was my reality at the time. My Director of Bands even warned me that I would be miserable if I decided to stay for a 4th year. I ended up taking my Fall 2015 semester off to try and figure out my next move...

Chapter 4: Delta State University My memory is a little foggy, but I think it was Summer of 2015 when I went to the International Trumpet Guild Conference with Robert Cheesman, my trumpet teacher at the time. This was an incredible experience where we got to hear numerous trumpet soloists and ensembles. It was here where Mr. Cheesman introduced me to Dr. Michael Ellzey. Fast forward a couple of months and Mr. Cheesman is telling me that I should heavily consider auditioning for Delta State University (which is where Dr. Ellzey recently started teaching). Of course I took his advice because he had become one of my biggest mentors during my time at Hinds CC. I ended up taking an audition and eventually began my adventure at DSU during the 2016 Spring Semester. Keep in mind that I ONLY considered attending this school because of a single individual, Dr. Ellzey. If you, the reader, happen to be of the age where you are applying for colleges, please do your due diligence and research every aspect of the institution that you may have any affiliation with. I did not do this. Instead, that same resentment that I began developing at Hinds CC only grew stronger and stronger. It didn't help that Cleveland, the town where DSU was located, was a mere thimble on a map and was 2hrs from any aspect of modern civilization. I'm not sure if you are familiar with the Mississippi Delta, but there is almost...nothing... there.

This also included any musical opportunities. I made it to the very end of my degree process where I had to do a semester of Student Teaching at a Middle/High School that the University selected from an approved list, but once I walked across that stage at graduation, I cut ties with music almost entirely. That was in 2019.

Chapter 5: From Music to Code
Once I graduated and began my transition away from music, I began to experience a wave of uncertainty that, like a tsunami, would rush in, recede, and just when things began to become clear, the wave would come rushing back. Music had become such a large part of my identity, that without it, I didn't really know what else I was "good at". For personal reasons, I moved to a small town in Arkansas which was larger than Cleveland, but was still absent of any real opportunities for anyone in their twenties hoping to dream big outside of the internet. My first job here was an associate at a Pilot Travel Center and then quickly transitioned to a Shift Supervisor at Starbucks. I started working at Starbucks just as the holiday themed items were making their way to the menu in October of 2019.

One of my best friends started her own Tech Startup in February of 2020 and brought me on as a part of her Social Media Marketing Team. For a short time, this seemed like the start to something great that just might "stick". Our CTO at the time caught wind that I had an interest in coding. He introduced me to a Udemy course by Brad Traversy on the topic of Bootstrap. I was LOVING IT. I slowly felt like I was starting to find my next "thing"! Obviously, I was a complete beginner so I had my limitations. I was tasked with smaller jobs such as designing and developing a page dedicated to our FAQ's so that I could build upon prior knowledge.

For reasons that I cannot disclose, the company separated from our CTO and I quickly found myself having to create my own questions and find my own answers. I started to find other YouTubers such as DevelopedbyEd, The Net Ninja, Lama Dev, Kevin Powell, JavaScript Mastery, and many others that made taking the time to learn to code less of a chore and more of a reward. I may write a post about the similarities that I have found between studying music and studying code, but for now, the important thing to know is that when you're studying music, you have your Professor that you meet with once a week to learn and refine the fundamentals of your instrument, etudes and other repertoire. It was almost impossible to deviate from the "syllabus" which, for people like me, was sort of a blessing in disguise because once I took on the challenge of learning code on my own, I found that I just wanted to learn everything all at once. I wish I had a syllabus to keep me on track. Looking back on the last two years, I feel like I have wasted so much trying to figure certain things out on my own or which "stack" is the best fit for me at this point in my coding journey. I will say that, although my thoughts may be negative, the fulfillment that I feel for getting to where I am now seems worth it. Though I still have my periods of days or weeks where motivation seems so very foreign, I am becoming more comfortable and confident in my abilities. I also have to remind myself that I was a music student for over 12 years and I have only been coding for 2 years. Honestly if you only count consecutive days that I've actually practiced code, it would be far less than 2 years. This pretty much gets us to the present! From here I would like to start routinely creating shorter posts geared towards code. There are a million and one different ways to explain concepts and fundamentals, so I am going to try and create rather than replicate. If you made it to the end, then I salute you!

Conclusion:
I know that the majority of this post was centered around my life and music rather than code, but it would be a fictional tale if I tried to tell it otherwise. I also recognize that this post may never be seen by another pair of eyes, but if that turns out to be false and you are uncertain of the stability in your current path in life, know that you're not alone. I'm always open to talk :)

Until next time!

Andrew T. Ladd
Creator of ALStudio

Items.Title: CSS对齐技术 | CSS Alignment techniques
Items.Description:
我整理了一个codepen 集合来展示不同的技术。

集合中的每个 codepen 都显示一个网格，该网格表示包含便笺的板，并且注释显示在板的不同位置，如下图所示：

对齐技术：

  • 利润
  • 线高
  • 表格单元格
  • 职位
  • 柔性

利润

使用margin属性很容易将元素水平居中，但是，对于垂直对齐，您必须计算高度或使用calc函数。

这些是需要的属性：

  • margin-top
  • margin-right
  • margin-bottom
  • margin-left

或简写属性margin 。

这是代码笔。

线高

使用line-height将完美地对齐元素，但是，如果文本占用超过一行的元素，它将不适合。

所需属性：

  • 这是代码笔。

    表格单元格

    使用display: table-cell垂直对齐不受字体大小或行高的影响，但作为一个缺点，该技术仅适用于内联元素。

    所需属性：

    • display: table-cell
    • text-align
    • vertical-align

    这是代码笔。

    职位

    这是用于定位元素的最常用技术之一

    所需属性：

    • position: relative
    • position: absolute
    • top
    • right
    • bottom
    • left
    • transform: translate(x, y)

    这是代码笔。

    柔性

    容器所需的属性：

    • display: flex
    • justify-content
    • align-items

    这是代码笔。

    结论

    应用的最佳技术取决于您的用例和页面架构。

    最后但同样重要的是，这篇文章的想法来自我在Platzi上的CSS Grid课程中所做的笔记，如果你会说或懂西班牙语，我强烈推荐这些笔记。



原文:

One common tasks that is requested often in web development is to align elements using CSS, this might sound simple, but since there is not one specific way to do it, it can become confusing.

I have put together a codepen collection to show the different techniques.

Each codepen in the collection shows a grid that represents a board that contains sticky-notes and the notes are displayed in different places of the board, like the following image:

Alignment techniques:

  • Margin
  • Line-height
  • Table-cell
  • Positions
  • Flex

Margin

Using the margin attribute it is easy to center the elements horizontally, however, for vertical alignment you would have to calculate the height or use the calc function.

These are the properties needed:

  • margin-top
  • margin-right
  • margin-bottom
  • margin-left

Or the shorthand property margin.

Here is the codepen.

Line-height

Using line-height will align the elements perfectly, however, if the text takes more than one line of the element it won't fit.

Properties needed:

  • text-align -> For horizontal alignment.
  • vertical-align -> For vertical alignment.
  • line-height -> defines the size of the line.

Here is the codepen.

Table-cell

Using display: table-cell the vertical alignment is not affected by font size or line height, but as a disadvantage this technique only applies to inline elements.

Properties needed:

  • display: table-cell
  • text-align
  • vertical-align

Here is the codepen.

Positions

This is one of the most common techniques used for positioning elements

Properties needed:

  • position: relative
  • position: absolute
  • top
  • right
  • bottom
  • left
  • transform: translate(x, y)

Here is the codepen.

Flex

The display: flex property was introduced with CSS3 and makes very simple and intuitive to align the elements, additionally this technique is more friendly with different writing forms, this is useful if you are looking to show your page in countries with different writing forms.

Properties needed for containers:

  • display: flex
  • justify-content
  • align-items

Here is the codepen.

Conclusion

The best technique to apply would depend on your use case and your page's architecture.

Last but no least, the idea for this entry came from the notes I took in the CSS Grid course on Platzi, that I highly recommend if you speak or understand Spanish.
